logs.empty: No logs yet. Reproduce the issue and entries will appear here.

help.title: Help
help.intro: Live Translator translates speech during calls and voice messages. Here's how it works and how to set it up in a couple of minutes.

howItWorks.heading: How it works
howItWorks.body: Your microphone doesn't go straight into Zoom/Meet — it goes into this app, which translates your speech and feeds the translation into the VB-CABLE virtual cable that your call app uses as its microphone. The peer's audio is captured from their app, translated, and played to your headphones.
howItWorks.out: Outgoing: your mic → translation → VB-CABLE → the peer hears the translation.
howItWorks.in: Incoming: the peer's audio → translation → your headphones (original is ducked).

quickStart.heading: Quick start
steps: Install VB-CABLE (button below) and restart your computer.
In Settings, choose your microphone and output device (headphones recommended).
In Zoom / Meet / Teams, set the microphone to “CABLE Output (VB-Audio Virtual Cable)”.
On the Live screen, pick the peer's audio source (their app, or “All system audio”).
Choose the languages and press Start.

micSetup.heading: Important: the mic in Zoom / Meet / Teams
micSetup.body: For the peer to hear the translation, set the call's microphone to “CABLE Output (VB-Audio Virtual Cable)” — not your regular mic. This is the key step; without it the translation has nowhere to go.
micSetup.note: When translation is off but the call's mic is set to CABLE Output, the app passes your normal voice through to the cable by default so you're still audible (can be turned off in Settings → Audio).

cable.heading: VB-CABLE — the virtual audio cable
cable.body: The free VB-CABLE tool creates a virtual microphone the app uses to deliver the translation into Zoom/Meet. Install it once and restart your computer.

voice.heading: Voice messages
voice.body: Drop an audio file (e.g. a WhatsApp voice message) into the window to get a transcript and translation. Or record with the mic button: it's translated, voiced, and saved as an .ogg file you can drag back into WhatsApp. The speech voice is chosen in Settings.

items.a: Check that the call's mic is set to “CABLE Output”, VB-CABLE is installed, and a translation session is running (or idle mic passthrough is enabled).

items.q: I can't hear the peer's translation
items.a: Check the output device in Settings and its volume. Make sure the correct peer audio source is selected on the Live screen.
Use headphones instead of speakers — otherwise the peer's audio leaks back into your mic.

items.q: Translation won't start or shows an error
items.a: Check your Gemini key's balance and limits in Google AI Studio and your internet connection. The error text on the Live screen names the cause.

apiKey.sectionDesc: A Gemini key is required to translate speech and voice messages. It's stored only on this computer.

audio.sectionDesc: Microphone — your voice to be translated. Output device — where you hear the peer's translation (headphones recommended to avoid echo).
audio.deviceMissing: Saved device not found — using the system default
audio.idlePassthrough: Pass the mic through to VB-CABLE when translation is off
audio.idlePassthroughHint: When no translation session is running, your normal voice goes straight to VB-CABLE so the peer still hears you — untranslated. Saves switching the mic in Zoom/Meet between talking and translating.

translation.sectionDesc: Fine-tuning for live translation. The defaults work well for most calls.
translation.echoTargetLanguage: Don't translate the peer when they speak my language
translation.echoTargetLanguageHint: When the peer switches to your language, you hear their original audio instead of a translation. Applies to the incoming direction only (peer → you).
translation.idleAutoStopHint: Automatically stop translating after 2 minutes of silence, so you don't spend Gemini minutes if you forget to stop the session.
translation.duckingEnabled: Lower the peer's volume while translating
translation.duckingEnabledHint: While a translation is playing, the peer app's original volume is automatically lowered so the translation is clearer, then restored afterwards.
translation.mixOriginalHint: The peer hears your translation plus your original voice quietly underneath — keeps your intonation. Original loudness is set below.
translation.vadEconomyHint: Doesn't send silence to Gemini — saves up to ~50%, but may clip the very start of a phrase

voice.sectionDesc: Settings for translating voice messages (recording and dropped-in files).
voice.ttsVoiceHint: The voice used to speak the translation of recorded messages. Voices differ in timbre and gender — e.g. Puck and Charon sound male, Kore and Zephyr female.

voiceClone.sectionDesc: Voice the translation of your recorded messages in your own ElevenLabs cloned voice. The API key is stored only on this computer; the voice id in settings.
voiceClone.useClone: Use my cloned voice for recorded messages
voiceClone.useCloneHint: When on, recorded voice messages are voiced by ElevenLabs using the voice id below. When off, the built-in Gemini voice is used.
voiceClone.getVoiceId: Where do I find my Voice ID?
voiceClone.needConfig: Enter and check a valid key + voice id to enable

key.heading: Step 1. Gemini API Key
key.desc: Enter your Gemini key to enable speech recognition and translation.
key.ready: Key is valid — you can continue

cable.heading: Step 2. VB-CABLE Virtual Cable
cable.desc: VB-CABLE is a virtual audio device that routes the translated speech into Zoom, WhatsApp, and other apps.
cable.detectedDesc: The virtual cable was detected. You can continue.
cable.installingHint: The VB-Audio installer will open. Click "Install Driver" and confirm the administrator-rights prompt.
cable.errDownload: Failed to download VB-CABLE. Check your internet connection or download it manually.
cable.errNoInstaller: Installer not found in the archive. Try downloading manually.
cable.rebootHint: The cable isn't detected yet — a reboot may be required.

devices.heading: Step 3. Audio Devices
devices.desc: Choose your microphone and output device. Headphones are recommended to avoid echo.

test.heading: Step 4. Test
test.instruction: Say a phrase — you'll hear it translated into the peer's language.
test.zoomBody: In Zoom/WhatsApp, select the microphone: CABLE Output (VB-Audio Virtual Cable).
test.zoomNote: The translator must be running (the "Start" button on the main screen).
test.finishFailed: Could not complete setup. Please try again.

voice.title: Voice Messages
voice.emptyHint:
No voice messages yet.
Record a voice message with the Record button or drag and drop an audio file (.ogg, .mp3, .wav, etc.).
Drag the translated file into WhatsApp when ready.

stageError.el_no_key: No ElevenLabs API key — add it in Settings
stageError.el_no_voice: No ElevenLabs voice id — add it in Settings
stageError.el_synth_failed: ElevenLabs synthesis failed — check key, voice and quota

voice.dragDisclaimer: Will arrive as an audio file, not a voice note

history.confirmClearBody: This will permanently delete all call transcripts and voice messages (including audio files).

live.transcriptEmpty: Transcript will appear here once the session starts
live.confirmClearBody: This removes the current on-screen transcript. The saved call history is not affected.
live.noAppsHint: No apps with audio — start an app and reopen the list
live.alertNoKeyDesc: Add a Gemini API key to start translation.
live.alertNoCableDesc: VB-CABLE virtual audio device is required for live translation.
live.alertEchoDesc: The selected output device does not look like headphones. Using speakers may cause echo.
live.costTooltip: Estimated cost at ~$0.037/min per direction (input $0.0053 + output $0.0315). A two-way call runs both sessions.
live.autoStopped: Translation stopped automatically after 2 minutes of silence to save Gemini credits. Press Start to resume.

error.noApiKey: Add an API key in Settings
error.cableMissing: VB-CABLE not found — run Setup Wizard
error.noAppSelected: Select an app to capture first
error.quotaExceeded: Gemini API quota or balance exhausted. Check your quotas and billing in Google AI Studio.
error.authFailed: Gemini API key is invalid or lacks access. Check the key in Settings.
error.modelUnavailable: The translation model isn't available for your key (no access to gemini-3.5-live-translate-preview).
error.connectFailed: Couldn't connect to Gemini. Check your internet connection.
error.noResponse: Gemini accepted the connection but never answered the session setup (model access or account may be the cause).

live.langLockedHint: Languages are locked during a session — stop the translation first
